policy_statement: Minister must take all reasonable steps to prepare, communicate, maintain and update successive NDCs, pursue domestic mitigation measures, and communicate an NDC every 5 years with progression and highest possible ambition, including specified accounting and reporting information.
mechanism_description: The Minister must take reasonable steps to prepare, communicate, maintain, and periodically update NDCs and to pursue domestic mitigation measures to achieve those NDC objectives, supporting an ongoing pathway for emissions reductions.
primary_causal_pathway: Governance set (NDC cycle) → mitigation targets + domestic measures → ↓ GHG emissions → ↓ atmospheric forcing
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Changed activity/system: National governance processes for climate action—set preparation, communication, maintenance, and periodic updating of NDCs, plus pursuit of domestic mitigation measures to meet stated objectives.  
2) Mediating process: Policy commitment and iterative planning/ratcheting create durable targets, accountability, and coordination that trigger or strengthen implementing regulations/investments across emitting sectors.  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Reduced national greenhouse gas emissions (and/or avoided growth in emissions) relative to baseline trajectories, contributing to lower cumulative emissions.
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect of policies using this mechanism is enabling and accelerating economy-wide GHG emissions reductions by mandating progressively updated NDC targets and associated domestic mitigation measures.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: The core mechanism links mandated NDC preparation/update and pursuit of domestic mitigation measures to economy-wide emissions reductions.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While governance processes can indirectly support adaptation or resource efficiency, the stated objective and principal outcome is mitigation via emissions-reduction targets and measures; any efficiency benefits are subordinate channels within mitigation implementation.  
3) Edge cases applied: If an NDC-focused law were purely procedural with no requirement to pursue mitigation measures (or focused primarily on adaptation components), classification could be ambiguous; here, the mechanism explicitly includes pursuing domestic mitigation measures to achieve NDC objectives, making mitigation dominant.  
4) Confirm applies to ALL instances: Any policy sharing this mechanism (NDC preparation/update linked to pursuing mitigation measures) is principally aimed at reducing GHG emissions through national target-setting and implementation cycles.  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether the policy should be further tagged (e.g., “governance/MRV,” “target-setting,” “planning,” “accountability”) depends on policy-level evidence and is deferred.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: Edge case: Some NDC-cycle mandates can be largely procedural and have uncertain real-world emissions impact if unenforced; nonetheless, the intended and dominant climate pathway remains mitigation via enabling/mandating emissions-reduction measures. Also, NDCs can include adaptation content, but this mechanism explicitly foregrounds mitigation measures to meet NDC objectives.
key_indicators: “must… take all reasonable steps”; “prepare, communicate and maintain successive NDCs”; “pursue domestic mitigation measures”; “communicate a NDC every 5 years”; “progression… reflects… highest possible ambition”
instance_edge_case_notes: Could also be interpreted as a regulatory mandate (legal obligation on ministerial process), but best fit is target-setting because the core requirement is establishing/updating national targets/commitments (NDCs) on a fixed cycle with progression/ambition.
